story: Passing through Landover, I saw toys with no children. Exhausted, I took the only motel room; the wiry manager warned me with a proverb about those who don’t heed advice. Next day: an empty bus route, dead school halls, teachers drinking—still no kids. While I wandered, the manager opened my room with a master key, spread the contents of my bag on the bed, and called the bald man in mirrored shades; three white-haired women crossed the lot to wait. That night at Wednesdays bar the bald man said, “See you soon,” and a stain on the floor seemed to skitter. In the parking lot, something moved inside an old Fiat; my fob failed as its door creaked open.
implausible_knowledge: While I wandered, the manager opened my room with a master key, spread the contents of my bag on the bed, and called the bald man in mirrored shades; three white-haired women crossed the lot to wait.
question: After fleeing the bar parking lot and getting his car moving, where is he most likely to head next?
answer_with_knowledge: Drive straight out of town without returning to the motel.
answer_without_knowledge: Drive back to his motel room.
